title: docloom: LLM to Documents, Instantly
subtitle: Validated IR, Deterministic Renderers, 6 Output Formats

slides.title: docloom: LLM to Documents, Instantly
slides.subtitle: Validated IR + Deterministic Renderers = 6 Native Formats
slides.notes: Welcome to docloom. This deck is authored using docloom itselfâ€”a dogfood test. We're going to walk through the problem we solve, the architecture, and how you can use it today.

slides.title: From Raw LLM Output to Production Files

items.text: LLM outputs are unstructured and unpredictable
Manual formatting is error-prone and time-consuming
Renderers need consistent, validated input
Quality checks should happen before any file is written
Users need native, editable formatsâ€”not rasterized images

slides.notes: The problem: LLMs are good at generating text, but not at producing structured output that renders correctly every time. We needed a bridge.

slides.title: Validated IR, Deterministic Renderers

items.text: LLM emits schema-validated JSON (Pydantic IR)
Deterministic linter checks layout, citations, contrast
Findings loop back to model for self-correction
Deterministic renderers: same IR = same output, always

blocks.text: The IR is deliberately non-recursive with plain tagged unionsâ€”shaped to survive real LLM structured-output constraints.

slides.notes: The core insight: put quality gates in the right place. Validate early, let the linter help the model correct itself, then render deterministically.

slides.title: Data Flow: From Raw Sources to Rendered Output

blocks.title: docloom architecture: sources to finished documents

nodes.label: 6 Formats
nodes.sublabel: PPTX DOCX XLSX PDF HTML MD

blocks.caption: The model emits validated structure; deterministic renderers produce every format.

items.text: Sources are chunked, embedded, and ranked, so only the most relevant evidence reaches the model
The model emits validated IR, never files; the linter checks layout and citations before any render
Deterministic renderers turn one IR into all six formats with no lossy conversion

slides.notes: The data flow: sources come in, get ingested and embedded, retrieval feeds grounded generation, the IR is validated, linted, and rendered. No hand-placed coordinatesâ€”the renderer owns the layout.

slides.title: Count What Matters: 14 Block Types, 6 Formats, 658+ Tests
slides.notes: Scale and scope: 14 block types cover everything from paragraphs to native charts to coordinate-free diagrams. 658 passing tests keep it all working.

slides.title: Generate Six Native Formats from One IR

items.text: PPTX: native editable charts, autofit text, native shapes
DOCX: hardened tables, repeating headers, fixed column widths
XLSX: typed cells, formulas, number formats
PDF: Typst compiled in-process, no LaTeX needed
HTML: semantic markup with WCAG 2.x contrast
Markdown: clean, readable prose

slides.notes: Same IR, six completely different renderers. Each owns its own coordinates and styling. Charts in PPTX are native editable objects, not rasterized images.

slides.title: Turn Source Data into Grounded Artifacts

items.text: Upload file, URL, or paste text
Free web research agent included
Hybrid BM25 + dense retrieval
Automatic chunking at 1000 chars
Chat with grounded, cited answers
5 one-click guides (briefing, FAQ, timeline)
6 artifact kinds: deck, doc, sheet, diagram, infographic, podcast

slides.notes: The studio brings it all together: import sources, chat with them, generate artifacts. Everything works offline with a local model.

slides.title: Keep Text Legible at Any Scale

items.text: Measured autofit: real text extents via FreeType against actual fonts
WCAG 2.x contrast ratio checking: 4.5:1 for normal text
Diagram legibility: labels never truncated, step down detail instead
Table hardening: fixed widths, repeating headers, split prevention
Geometric QA: off-slide detection, overlap checks, font sprawl

blocks.text: Design principle: lint before render. Only error severity blocks a render; warnings help improve.

slides.notes: Quality is not an afterthought. Every diagram label is tested for legibility. PPTX text is actually measured, not guessed.

slides.title: Local-First, Offline-Ready, Multi-Tenant-Safe

items.text: Runs fully offline against a local model (ollama, llama.cpp, etc.)
SQLite default, Postgres for scale
Session tokens stored only as SHA-256 hashes
Cross-tenant access returns 404, not 403
Secrets encrypted at rest with Fernet

slides.notes: The studio is designed for privacy and safety. You own your data. There's no third-party account required, no API keys sent to the cloud unless you choose.

slides.title: One Command to Launch

items.text: Installs dependencies, builds frontend, opens browser
Prerequisites: uv, Node 22+, npm

slides.notes: Getting started is simple. One launcher script handles everything. The first visit registers a local account; everything stays in your data directory.

slides.title: Set Up Providers, Storage, and Secrets

items.text: Generation: ollama, llama-server, lmstudio, openai, anthropic, gemini
Storage: SQLite (default) or Postgres via DOCLOOM_DB_URL
Secrets: Fernet encryption, masked in API responses
Optional extras: [pdf,diagrams] for engine; [podcast,ingest] for studio
Settings resolve: per-user â†’ global â†’ built-in defaults

slides.notes: Fully configurable. Switch providers in the UI without restarting. Add Postgres for multi-node deployments. All secrets are encrypted at rest.

slides.title: Design Principles: Built for Reliability

items.text: Validated IR, not code: LLM output is data, never executable
Deterministic renderers: same input = same output, always
Lint before render: findings can loop back to model
Nothing authored is lost: labels step down, never truncate
Graceful degradation: optional deps improve output, never crash

slides.notes: These principles drive every architectural decision. Schema validation prevents entire classes of bugs. Determinism means you can regenerate anytime and get the same file.

slides.title: Open Source, Local-First, Production-Ready
